resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def kthFactor(self, n: int, k: int) -> int:
        factors = [i for i in range(1, n + 1) if n % i == 0]
        if k > len(factors):
            return -1
        return factors[k - 1]
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a list comprehension to generate all factors of n in O(sqrt(n)) time. It then checks if k is within the bounds of the list, and returns the kth factor if it is. If k is out of bounds, it returns -1.
